title: 10 best herbal teas for gerd
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gerd are infusions of plants and spices that can help alleviate the symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). These teas can reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and improve digestion, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of gerd symptoms. Some examples of herbal teas that help with gerd include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, and marshmallow root. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while ginger tea can help stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals with gerd can experience relief from their symptoms, leading to improved quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. It also has calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract, which can help relieve symptoms of GERD such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm tea has been shown to relax the sphincter muscle that separates the esophagus from the stomach, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Furthermore, lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has been found to reduce gastric acid secretion, providing additional relief from GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from GERD symptoms. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Spasmolytic: Lemon balm tea can help relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, preventing spasms and reducing the risk of acid reflux. This is attributed to the presence of citral and citronellal.
Carminative: Lemon balm tea can help reduce gas and bloating, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like citral and geraniol.
Antioxidant: Lemon balm tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tea can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger GERD symptoms. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and eugenol, which have been shown to have calming effects on the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for GERD due to their antispasmodic properties that can help reduce symptoms of indigestion and acid reflux.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in soothing irritation in the digestive tract caused by GERD.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm tea plant can still contribute to the tea's soothing effects on the digestive system and help alleviate symptoms of GERD.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with GERD because it contains substances that can help balance the stomach's acid production. One of its key compounds, glycyrrhizic acid, can increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. Additionally, licorice tea may help improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, further alleviating GERD symptoms. However, it is essential to consume deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL) tea or licorice products with the glycyrrhizic acid removed, as high levels of this compound can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium depletion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from GERD symptoms, due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.
Mucosal protective: Licorice tea can help protect the mucous membrane of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing irritation from acid reflux, because of the compound glycyrrhizic acid.
Demulcent: Licorice tea forms a soothing film over the mucous membrane, alleviating pain and inflammation, thanks to its high mucilage content.
Antacid: Licorice tea can neutralize stomach acid, preventing the onset of GERD symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and coumarins.
Gastroprotective: Licorice tea can help maintain the integrity of the stomach lining, preventing damage from gastric acid, as a result of its triterpenoid saponins content.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for GERD due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritation in the esophagus.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are used in making medicinal tea for GERD as they contain glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce stomach acid production.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are used in medicinal tea for GERD because they can help relieve symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
Bark: The bark of the licorice tea plant is used in medicinal tea for GERD as it contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the stomach lining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with GERD because it has several properties that can soothe and protect the digestive tract. It contains volatile oils, such as anethole, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce spasms, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Fennel tea also has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by stomach acid. Additionally, fennel tea can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Its carminative properties can also help relieve gas and bloating, providing further relief for individuals with GERD.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel tea can help reduce bloating and gas by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, allowing for the expulsion of excess gas, thus alleviating symptoms of GERD. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils, mainly fenchone and estragole.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may help alleviate symptoms of GERD caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly anethole, fenchone, and limonene.

Antispasmodic: Fennel tea can help relieve smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux, a primary symptom of GERD. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, particularly estragole and fenchone.

Antioxidant: Fennel tea contains antioxidant compounds that can help protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress and damage, which may contribute to GERD symptoms. The primary antioxidant constituents in fennel tea include rosmarinic acid, quercetin, and various flavonoids.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel tea can help stimulate digestion and improve overall digestive function, which may help alleviate symptoms of GERD. This property is attributed to the presence of volatile oils, particularly anethole, fenchone, and limonene.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in volatile oils, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain flavonoids and antioxidants that can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
Stems: Fennel stems have a milder flavor than the seeds or leaves and can be used to make a soothing tea that helps reduce gas and bloating associated with GERD.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are a lesser-known source of the plant's beneficial compounds and can be used to make a mild, aromatic tea that helps promote healthy digestion and reduce GERD symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound tea helps with GERD because it contains natural compounds that can provide relief from symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can provide relief from heartburn and acid reflux. Horehound tea is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can help improve digestion and prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. Additionally, horehound tea can help relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, which can further prevent acid reflux and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horehound tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from GERD symptoms, due to the presence of marrubiin and flavonoids.
Expectorant: It helps clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and discomfort that can worsen GERD, thanks to its diterpenes and volatile oils.
Antispasmodic: Horehound tea can relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping, which can contribute to GERD, due to its volatile oils and bitter compounds.
Carminative: It helps prevent the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing bloating, discomfort, and the risk of acid reflux, attributed to its volatile oils and bitter principles.

Choleretic: Horehound tea stimulates the production of bile, aiding in digestion and the breakdown of fats, which can help prevent the overproduction of stomach acid and subsequent GERD symptoms, due to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Horehound tea made from leaves is often used to treat GERD due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation in the esophagus.
Flowers: The flowers of the horehound plant are used in tea to soothe digestion and alleviate symptoms of GERD, thanks to their mild sedative and antispasmodic effects.
Roots: Horehound root tea can help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux, a common symptom of GERD.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential oils that can stimulate digestion and provide relief from GERD symptoms, making them a valuable addition to horehound tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with GERD because it contains active compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds can help soothe the inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux and reduce the frequency and intensity of GERD symptoms. Additionally, thyme tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which may help prevent the overgrowth of harmful bacteria in the stomach that can contribute to GERD. Furthermore, thyme tea can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Drinking thyme tea after meals may provide relief from GERD symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, a common symptom of GERD, due to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and rosmarinic acid.

Antispasmodic: Thyme tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing spasms and discomfort, thanks to its antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to compounds like thymol and carvacrol.

Antimicrobial: Thyme tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, such as H. pylori, which can contribute to GERD symptoms, due to the presence of volatile oils like thymol and carvacrol.

Carminative: Thyme tea can help alleviate gas and bloating, common symptoms of GERD, by promoting the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, thanks to its carminative properties, which are attributed to its volatile oils.

Astringent: Thyme tea's astringent properties can help protect the mucous membrane of the esophagus by tightening and toning the tissues, providing relief from GERD symptoms, due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the inflammation in the esophagus caused by GERD.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain volatile oils, including thymol, which has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, potentially reducing the discomfort caused by GERD.
Stems: Thyme stems contain flavonoids that have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which can help prevent and alleviate GERD symptoms.

Seeds: Thyme seeds are a good source of essential oils, including carvacrol, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, potentially reducing the risk of infections that can exacerbate GERD.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marjoram tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. It can also help to relax the muscles that control the opening and closing of the esophageal sphincter, preventing acid reflux. Additionally, marjoram tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and reducing the symptoms of GERD. Its calming effect on the stomach and intestines can provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with the condition. Drinking marjoram tea regularly can help manage GERD symptoms and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from GERD symptoms. This is due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.

Spasmolytic: Marjoram tea helps relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, preventing spasms and alleviating GERD-related discomfort, thanks to its content of terpene hydrocarbons and flavonoids.

Carminative: Marjoram tea promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with GERD, due to its essential oil constituents like terpinene and terpineol.

Antioxidant: Marjoram tea contains antioxidants that protect the gastric lining from damage caused by free radicals, supporting overall gastrointestinal health and helping with GERD management. The antioxidant properties come from rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Marjoram tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the gastrointestinal tract, maintaining a healthy balance of gut flora and contributing to GERD symptom relief. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like terpinen-4-ol and γ-terpinene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the marjoram tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for GERD due to their anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can help soothe irritation in the digestive tract.

Flowers: The flowers of the marjoram tea plant are used in medicinal tea for GERD because they contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and provide relief from digestive discomfort.

Seeds: The seeds of the marjoram tea plant are used in medicinal tea for GERD due to their high concentration of thymol, which has antispasmodic properties that can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of GERD.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with GERD because it contains carnosol and carnosic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. Additionally, rosemary tea can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and reduce the production of stomach acid. This can help alleviate symptoms of GERD, such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach discomfort. Furthermore, rosemary tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the gastrointestinal system, which can help prevent the spasms and cramping that can worsen GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, a key symptom of GERD, due to the presence of compounds such as carnosol and carnosic acid.

Antispasmodic: Rosemary tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing spasms and cramping that can worsen GERD symptoms, thanks to its content of volatile oils such as 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.

Improves digestion: Rosemary tea stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, helping the body break down food and reducing the risk of acid reflux, due to its rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid content.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tea contains potent antioxidants that help protect the gastrointestinal lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of GERD complications, due to its high concentration of phenolic diterpenes and flavonoids.

Reduces stomach acid: Rosemary tea has been shown to reduce the production of stomach acid, which can help alleviate GERD symptoms, by activating the release of bile and promoting proper digestion, thanks to its active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for GERD due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relax the gastrointestinal muscles.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in medicinal tea for GERD to help soothe and heal the digestive tract.

Stems: The stems of the rosemary tea plant contain essential oils and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in medicinal tea for GERD to help reduce inflammation and promote digestion.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in medicinal tea for GERD to help protect the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Sage is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can improve digestion and help prevent acid reflux. Additionally, sage contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a protective effect on the lining of the stomach and esophagus, reducing the symptoms of GERD. Drinking sage tea regularly may help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with GERD, providing relief and improving overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from GERD symptoms, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in sage tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect the stomach lining from oxidative damage, which can contribute to GERD.
Astringent: Sage tea's astringent properties, caused by tannins, help tighten and strengthen the tissues in the esophagus, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effect of sage tea, due to its volatile oils like camphor and cineole, helps relax the gastrointestinal muscles, preventing spasms and acid reflux.

Carminative: Sage tea's carminative properties, from essential oils like thujone, help expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and pressure on the stomach, thereby alleviating GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the sage tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for GERD due to their anti-inflammatory and calming effects on the digestive system.

Flowers: The flowers of the sage tea plant are used in medicinal tea for GERD because they contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus.
Seeds: The seeds of the sage tea plant are used in medicinal tea for GERD because they contain thujone, which can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and improve digestion.
Bark: The bark of the sage tea plant is used in medicinal tea for GERD because it contains tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with GERD due to its natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. The tea is rich in citric acid, which can neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from acid reflux. Additionally, it contains flavonoids that help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid from backing up into the esophagus. The herbal tea is also caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing GERD symptoms. Drinking large-leaved lime tea can offer a natural and effective way to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with gastroesophageal reflux disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, a common symptom of GERD, due to its high content of flavonoids and triterpenes.
Mucosal protective: The tea creates a protective barrier on the mucous membrane of the esophagus, preventing further damage from gastric acid, thanks to its mucilage content.
Antioxidant: Large-leaved lime tea contains antioxidants that help protect the esophageal lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by GERD, owing to its rich polyphenol content.
Spasmolytic: The tea can help relax the smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating spasms and pain, due to its volatile oil content, including limonene and citral.

Astringent: Large-leaved lime tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the tissues in the esophagus, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of GERD, resulting from tannins present in the tea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, common symptoms of GERD.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, they can help protect the lining of the esophagus and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
Bark: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with GERD.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and can help protect the stomach lining, reducing the risk of acid reflux and damage caused by GERD.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the irritated esophageal lining caused by acid reflux. It contains mucilage, a type of fiber that creates a protective barrier in the stomach, preventing acid from splashing up into the esophagus. Plantain tea also has antacid effects, neutralizing stomach acid and promoting the healing of damaged tissue. Additionally, it helps to strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid from escaping the stomach. Drinking plantain tea can provide relief from GERD symptoms, such as heartburn, regurgitation, and difficulty swallowing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for GERD as it can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and irritation. Active constituents include flavonoids, tannins, and mucilage.

Antacid: Plantain tea has antacid properties that can neutralize stomach acid, which is beneficial for GERD as it can help reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux. The mucilage in plantain tea is responsible for its antacid properties.

Protective: Plantain tea forms a protective layer on the mucous membrane of the digestive tract, which can help prevent damage from stomach acid. This is important for GERD as it can help protect the esophagus from acid damage. The mucilage in plantain tea is responsible for its protective properties.

Astringent: Plantain tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone tissues in the digestive tract, which is important for GERD as it can help reduce inflammation and irritation. The tannins in plantain tea are responsible for its astringent properties.

Soothing: Plantain tea has soothing properties that can help calm and relax the digestive tract, which is important for GERD as it can help alleviate symptoms such as discomfort and pain. The mucilage in plantain tea is responsible for its soothing properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain tea plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for GERD due to their mucilage content, which can help protect and soothe the digestive tract.
Seeds: Plantain tea made from seeds is beneficial for GERD as they contain high levels of mucilage and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plantain tea plant are used in medicinal tea for GERD because they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.

Flowers: Plantain tea made from flowers is good for GERD because they contain flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract.
